title: Roast Pumpkin Soup
description: My Thermomix take on Donna Hay's pumpkin soup — velvety, warming and on the table in under half an hour.
intro: Donna Hay's pumpkin soup is a winter staple — simple, silky and comforting. The Thermomix cooks the pumpkin and blends it smooth in the same bowl, so it's barely any work and barely any washing up.
ingredients: 800 g pumpkin, peeled and cut into 3 cm cubes
steps: Place onion and garlic in the mixing bowl and chop 4 sec / speed 5. Scrape down.
Add the olive oil and sauté 3 min / 120°C / speed 1.
Add the pumpkin, stock and nutmeg. Cook 20 min / 100°C / speed 1.
Blend, increasing slowly 1 min / speed 9, until completely silky.
Add the coconut milk or cream and mix 20 sec / speed 4. Season and serve topped with pumpkin seeds.
tips: For Donna's deeper flavour, roast the pumpkin at 200°C for 20 minutes first, then add at step 3 and cut the cook time to 10 minutes.
Coconut milk keeps it dairy-free; cream makes it richer.
A pinch of chilli or grated ginger with the onion is a lovely lift.

faq.q: Can you cook pumpkin soup entirely in a Thermomix?
faq.a: Yes — it softens the pumpkin and blends it silky-smooth in the same bowl, so there's no separate pot or blender.

faq.q: How do I make it richer?
faq.a: Use cream instead of coconut milk, or roast the pumpkin first for a deeper, caramelised flavour.

faq.q: Is this the original Donna Hay recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Donna Hay and pumpkin soup; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
